title: Gowin BRAMS mapping using more DPB/SP's than expected in frame buffer
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.51 (git sha1 c4b5190229616f7ebf8197f43990b4429de3e420, g++ 14.2.1 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

1. Get the following Verilog file, which creates a frame buffer.
```verilog
module dpb_issue_example #(
    parameter RESOLUTION_X = 120,
    parameter RESOLUTION_Y = 68,
    localparam RES_X_WIDTH = $clog2(RESOLUTION_X),
    localparam RES_Y_WIDTH = $clog2(RESOLUTION_Y),
    parameter R_PIXEL_WIDTH = 5,
    parameter G_PIXEL_WIDTH = 6,
    parameter B_PIXEL_WIDTH = 5,
    localparam RGB_PIXEL_WIDTH = R_PIXEL_WIDTH + G_PIXEL_WIDTH + B_PIXEL_WIDTH,
) (
    input clk_in,
    input nread_write,
    input [RES_X_WIDTH-1:0] pixel_position_x,
    input [RES_Y_WIDTH-1:0] pixel_position_y,
    input [R_PIXEL_WIDTH-1:0] in_pixel_color_r,
    input [G_PIXEL_WIDTH-1:0] in_pixel_color_g,
    input [B_PIXEL_WIDTH-1:0] in_pixel_color_b,
    output [R_PIXEL_WIDTH-1:0] out_pixel_color_r,
    output [G_PIXEL_WIDTH-1:0] out_pixel_color_g,
    output [B_PIXEL_WIDTH-1:0] out_pixel_color_b
);
  reg[RGB_PIXEL_WIDTH-1:0] current_pixel_value;
  assign out_pixel_color_r = current_pixel_value[R_PIXEL_WIDTH-1:0];
  assign out_pixel_color_g = current_pixel_value[G_PIXEL_WIDTH-1+R_PIXEL_WIDTH:R_PIXEL_WIDTH];
  assign out_pixel_color_b = current_pixel_value[B_PIXEL_WIDTH-1+R_PIXEL_WIDTH+G_PIXEL_WIDTH
                                                :R_PIXEL_WIDTH+G_PIXEL_WIDTH];
  
  reg [RGB_PIXEL_WIDTH-1:0] frame_buffer[RESOLUTION_X][RESOLUTION_Y];

  always @(posedge (clk_in)) begin
    // Frame Buffer Acess Logic
    current_pixel_value <= 0;
    if(nread_write) begin
      frame_buffer[pixel_position_x][pixel_position_y] <= {in_pixel_color_b, in_pixel_color_g, in_pixel_color_r};
    end else begin
      // Output current pixel
      current_pixel_value <= frame_buffer[pixel_position_x][pixel_position_y];
    end
  end
  // ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
endmodule
```
2. Run the command `yosys -p "read_verilog -sv dpb_issue_example.v ; synth_gowin -top dpb_issue_example"`, and see that it uses 8 SP's.
3.  Run the command `yosys -p "read_verilog -sv dpb_issue_example.v ; synth_gowin -top dpb_issue_example" | grep Warning`, and see the multiple warnings related a out of bounds selection in `yosys/gowin/brams_map.v`.

### Expected Behavior

With a BRAMS (SP) being able to store 16k words of 32bits in the gowin primitives (https://cdn.gowinsemi.com.cn/UG285E.pdf), I expected that a matrix of 120x68=8160 pixels, with each pixel using 16bits, would only need a single Single Port BRAMS.

### Actual Behavior

The mapper ends up using more BRAMS than expected:
```bash

2.30. Printing statistics.

=== dpb_issue_example ===

   Number of wires:                 67
   Number of wire bits:            407
   Number of public wires:          67
   Number of public wire bits:     407
   Number of ports:                 10
   Number of port bits:             48
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                 86
     ALU                            22
     GND                             1
     IBUF                           32
     LUT2                            6
     OBUF                           16
     SP                              8
     VCC                             1

2.31. Executing CHECK pass (checking for obvious problems).
Checking module dpb_issue_example...
Found and reported 0 problems.

Warnings: 4 unique messages, 4 total
End of script. Logfile hash: 0d7176a733, CPU: user 0.47s system 0.01s, MEM: 58.82 MB peak
Yosys 0.51 (git sha1 c4b5190229616f7ebf8197f43990b4429de3e420, g++ 14.2.1 -fPIC -O3)
Time spent: 35% 8x techmap (0 sec), 16% 20x read_verilog (0 sec), ...
```
And outputs several warnings related to out of range bit mapping.
```bash
> yosys -p "read_verilog -sv dpb_issue_example.v ; synth_gowin -top dpb_issue_example" | grep Warning
/nix/store/i167d4cjdxv18f9l8x41xhd0h3yhfaf3-yosys-0.51/bin/../share/yosys/gowin/brams_map.v:112: Warning: Range [7:0] select out of bounds on signal `\PORT_A_WR_DATA': Setting 6 MSB bits to undef.
/nix/store/i167d4cjdxv18f9l8x41xhd0h3yhfaf3-yosys-0.51/bin/../share/yosys/gowin/brams_map.v:112: Warning: Range select [16:9] out of bounds on signal `\PORT_A_WR_DATA': Setting all 8 result bits to undef.
/nix/store/i167d4cjdxv18f9l8x41xhd0h3yhfaf3-yosys-0.51/bin/../share/yosys/gowin/brams_map.v:112: Warning: Range select [25:18] out of bounds on signal `\PORT_A_WR_DATA': Setting all 8 result bits to undef.
/nix/store/i167d4cjdxv18f9l8x41xhd0h3yhfaf3-yosys-0.51/bin/../share/yosys/gowin/brams_map.v:112: Warning: Range select [34:27] out of bounds on signal `\PORT_A_WR_DATA': Setting all 8 result bits to undef.
ABC: Warning: The network is combinational.
Warnings: 4 unique messages, 4 total
```

Here is the full log: [log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19413830/log.txt)
